name: Route of the Villages
name_original: Rota das Aldeias — percurso circular PR 9 SNT no Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais
description: Jog the Rota das Aldeias, a moderate 7.8-mile (12.6 km) circular route in Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais, exploring rural villages and a Roma
region_intro: Jogging around São João Das Lampas offers diverse terrain, from coastal paths to forested trails, making it suitable for various running preferences. The region, part of the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park, features a mix of natural landscapes including hills and varied elevations. Its proximity to the Atlantic coast provides opportunities for runs with ocean views. This area is characterized by its natural beauty and a network of trails. Best jogging routes around São João Das Lampas The most popular jogging route is Route of the Villages, a 7.8 miles (12.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 27 minutes to complete. This route connects local communities through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local runners is Aguda Beach – View of the Atlantic Ocean loop from Duna Consolidada da Praia do Magoito, a moderate 5.6 miles (9.0 km) path. This route offers coastal views and diverse terrain. Local runners also love the Praia da Samarra loop from São João das Lampas e Terrugem, a 10.0 miles (16.1 km) trail leading through coastal and inland paths, often completed in about 1 hour 58 minutes. Jogging around São João Das Lampas is defined by varied terrain, coastal paths, and forested trails, with routes available for different ability levels. The routes in São João Das Lampas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore São João Das Lampas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in São João Das Lampas?
A: São João Das Lampas offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 45 trails available. These routes cater to various fitness levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options.

Q: What makes São João Das Lampas a great destination for joggers?
A: The region's diverse landscape, including its proximity to the &lt;strong&gt;Sintra-Cascais Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, varied terrain, and coastal access, makes it ideal for jogging. You'll find a mix of forested paths, scenic coastal trails, and routes with varied elevations, all within a mild climate that's enjoyable year-round.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in São João Das Lampas?
A: Yes, São João Das Lampas has several easy jogging routes perfect for beginners. There are at least 3 routes classified as easy, offering a gentle introduction to the area's beautiful scenery without overly challenging terrain.

Q: Can I find challenging running trails for experienced athletes?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a more rigorous workout, São João Das Lampas features 13 difficult running trails. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19413435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia da Samarra loop from São João das Lampas e Terrugem&lt;/a&gt;, a 10.0-mile (16.1 km) route with significant elevation changes, often completed in under two hours.

Q: Are there circular running routes in the area?
A: Many of the running routes in São João Das Lampas are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10344912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguda Beach – View of the Atlantic Ocean loop from Duna Consolidada da Praia do Magoito&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 5.6-mile (9.0 km) loop offering coastal views.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while jogging in São João Das Lampas?
A: Jogging in São João Das Lampas allows you to discover several natural highlights. You might encounter stunning coastal features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/463773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ursa Beach&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy the tranquility of forested areas such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/338832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pena Park&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes may even lead you past picturesque spots like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8001951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall Lake&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the jogging trails in São João Das Lampas dog-friendly?
A: Many of the natural trails and paths in the São João Das Lampas region, particularly within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife, and to check specific route regulations before you go.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in São João Das Lampas?
A: The running routes in São João Das Lampas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 25 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful coastal views, and the opportunity to explore both natural landscapes and local villages, such as on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381093076/route-of-the-villages" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Villages&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there jogging routes accessible by public transport?
A: While many routes are best accessed by car, some trails may be reachable via local bus services connecting to São João Das Lampas and nearby villages. It's advisable to check local public transport schedules and stops in relation to your chosen starting point before planning your run.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in São João Das Lampas?
A: Thanks to Portugal's mild climate, jogging in São João Das Lampas is enjoyable for most of the year. Spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures, while summer mornings and evenings are ideal for coastal runs. Even winter can be suitable, with cooler, crisp air for invigorating runs.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the running routes?
A: Yes, the diverse landscape of São João Das Lampas offers several opportunities for stunning views. Many coastal routes provide panoramic vistas of the Atlantic Ocean, and trails within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park can lead to elevated spots with expansive views, such as near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/131009" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo da Roca&lt;/a&gt;, the westernmost point of mainland Europe.

Q: Can I find jogging routes that pass by cafes or places to rest?
A: Many routes in São João Das Lampas connect local communities, offering opportunities to pass by cafes or small shops for refreshments. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381093076/route-of-the-villages" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Villages&lt;/a&gt;, for example, connects various local communities where you can often find places to rest and refuel.
tips: The Main Church of São João Batista, in São João das Lampas, is a late-medieval construction. Documented since the 15th century, it may have origins in the 13th or 14th centuries. It has an outstanding Manueline portal from the time of King D. Manuel I and is classified as a Monument of Public Interest.

The Roman Bridge of Catribana, near the village of Catribana in São João das Lampas, was identified as Roman only in the 1980s and restored in 2019. Built with blocks of local limestone, crossing the Bolelas stream, it probably connected a Roman villa to a main road or two important villas.
Small lagoon in the village of Assafora. There is a small square next to the lake where some ducks and geese approach if they suspect there is bread nearby. Very peaceful place.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Rota das Aldeias?
faqs.answer: The Rota das Aldeias is approximately 12.6 kilometers long. For jogging, komoot estimates an average duration of about 1 hour and 27 minutes, though this can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or less experienced joggers?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Rota das Aldeias (PR 9 SNT) is characterized by a low technical difficulty, making it very suitable for beginners or those new to trail jogging. Its relatively moderate elevation changes and varied terrain offer an enjoyable experience for most fitness levels.

faqs.question: Where does the Rota das Aldeias trail start, and what are the parking options?
faqs.answer: The Rota das Aldeias is a circular route that begins and ends in the village of São João das Lampas. You can typically find parking within the village itself, which serves as a convenient starting point for the loop.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse terrain, primarily consisting of dirt roads and footpaths, which provide a natural running surface. You'll also encounter sections within villages that use asphalt roads. The trail navigates through agricultural areas, pine forests, and ancient rural roads, some with Roman origins.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Rota das Aldeias?
faqs.answer: As you jog, you'll experience picturesque village landscapes and traditional countryside views. Notable sights include the Church of São João Baptista de São João das Lampas, the recently restored Catribana Roman Bridge, and the serene Assafora Lagoon. You'll also pass by old windmills and fields with tall grass, showcasing the rural 'saloia' heritage.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or shops along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route bypasses or passes through villages such as Cortezia and Assafora. In these villages, you can find amenities like cafes, restaurants, and shops, perfect for a quick break or to refuel.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The Rota das Aldeias can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures. Summer can be warm, so an early morning or late afternoon jog is recommended. Winter can be wet, but the trail is generally well-maintained.

faqs.question: Is the Rota das Aldeias dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Rota das Aldeias is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through agricultural areas and villages, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially near livestock or in populated areas, to ensure their safety and respect for local regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to access the trail or Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or to pay an entrance fee to access the Rota das Aldeias trail or the Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais itself. The park and its trails are freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of other official trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Trilho das Areias, the EuroVelo 1 - Atlantic Coast Route - part Portugal 10, and sections of the Caminho dos Cabecinhos.

faqs.question: What should I pack or wear for jogging this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain of dirt roads, footpaths, and some asphalt, comfortable trail running shoes are ideal. Light, breathable athletic wear is recommended. Depending on the season, consider bringing a light jacket or rain gear. Always carry water, and perhaps a small snack, especially if you plan to jog the entire route.

waypoints.name: Church of São João Baptista de São João das Lampas
